person.name: Ludwig IV von Thüringen
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Ludwig IV von Thüringen
Born the eldest son of Hermann I and Sophie of Bavaria. He married Elisabeth of Hungary in 1221 who bore him three children. 

In 1227 he traveled to Italy to support the emperor in a crusade. Before leaving for the Holy Land he fell ill and died. 

He was first buried in Otranto but was later brought home to Thuringia to be buried at Reinhardsbrunn. Soon after the burial wonders occurred at the grave and Louis was venerated as as Saint. 

He was never canonized though and the worship came to an halt at the beginning of the 14th century.

sourceList.title: Ludwig IV of Thuringia, „Find A Grave Index“
Foundation for Medieval Genealogy - LUDWIG (28 Oct 1200-Otranto 11 Sep 1227).

zh.lifeSummary: 當Ludwig IV von Thüringen於1200年10月28日出生在德國賀林根瓦特包可萊斯可茲包格時，他的父親Hermann I. von Thüringen45歲，母親Sophia von Wittelsbach30歲。他於1221年與Landgräfin Elisabeth von Ungarn die Heilige在德國賀林根艾森亞賀結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1227年9月11日在義大利普格利亞特拉 達特朗托奧特朗托享年26歲，埋葬在德國賀林根。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Thüringen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German (Thüringer): habitational name for someone from Thuringia. The region is named from its former occupation by the T(h)uringii, an ancient Germanic tribe. Compare Deringer and Dieringer .

variants.name: Deringer
meanings: from a short form of an old personal name, a variant of Därr (see Darr ).
variant of Döringer, a habitational name for someone from Thuringia (see Doering ). Compare Derringer .

nameMeanings.name: Ludwig
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from hlud ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’; an ancient royal and imperial name. See Louis .

variants.name: Louis
meanings: name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hlōd ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’. It was very common in French royal and noble families. Louis I ( 778–840 ) was the son of Charlemagne, who ruled as both King of France and Holy Roman Emperor. Altogether, the name was borne by sixteen kings of France up to the French Revolution, in which Louis XVI perished. Louis XIV, ‘the Sun King’ ( 1638–1715 ), reigned for seventy-two years ( 1643–1715 ), presiding in the middle part of his reign over a period of unparalleled French power and prosperity. In modern times Louis is also found in the English-speaking world (usually pronounced ‘loo-ee’). In Britain the Anglicized form Lewis is rather more common, whereas in America the reverse is true.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Vivian   and   Vivien .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Ludwig IV von Thüringen was born on 28 October 1200, in Creuzburg, Wartburgkreis, Thuringia, Germany, his father, Hermann I. von Thüringen, was 45 and his mother, Sophia von Wittelsbach, was 30. He married Landgräfin Elisabeth von Ungarn die Heilige in 1221, in Eisenach, Thuringia, Germany. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 11 September 1227, in Otranto, Terra d'Otranto, Puglia, Italy, at the age of 26, and was buried in Thuringia, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ludwig IV von Thüringen est né le 28 octobre 1200, à Creuzburg, Wartburgkreis, Thüringen, Allemagne, son père, Hermann I. von Thüringen, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Sophia von Wittelsbach, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Landgräfin Elisabeth von Ungarn die Heilige en 1221, à Eisenach, Thüringen, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 11 septembre 1227, à Otranto, Terra d'Otranto, Puglia, Italie, à l’âge de 26 ans, et a été enterré à Thüringen, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ludwig IV von Thüringen am 28. Oktober 1200 in Creuzburg, Wartburgkreis, Thüringen, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hermann I. von Thüringen 45 und seine Mutter Sophia von Wittelsbach 30. Er hat 1221 in Eisenach, Thüringen, Deutschland Landgräfin Elisabeth von Ungarn die Heilige geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 11. September 1227 im Alter von 26 Jahren in Otranto, Terra d'Otranto, Puglia, Italien und wurde in Thüringen, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ludwig IV von Thüringen è nato il il 28 ottobre 1200 nel Creuzburg, Wartburgkreis, Thüringen, Germania, il suo padre, Hermann I. von Thüringen, aveva 45 anni e il suo madre, Sophia von Wittelsbach, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Landgräfin Elisabeth von Ungarn die Heilige nel 1221, nel Eisenach, Thüringen, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 11 settembre 1227, nel Otranto, Terra d'Otranto, Puglia, Italia, all'età di 26 anni, e fu sepolto a Thüringen, Germania.

ko.lifeSummary: Ludwig IV von Thüringen(이)가 1200년 10월 28일 독일 스u링 워트버그크레이스 크레우즈버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hermann I. von Thüringen(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Sophia von Wittelsbach(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1221년 독일 스u링 아이센악h에서 Landgräfin Elisabeth von Ungarn die Heilige(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1227년 9월 11일 이탈리아 푸그리야 터라 드'트랜토 트랜토에서 26세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 스u링에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ludwig IV von Thüringen nasceu em 28 de outubro de 1200, em Creuzburg, Wartburgkreis, Thüringen, Alemanha, seu pai, Hermann I. von Thüringen, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Sophia von Wittelsbach, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Landgräfin Elisabeth von Ungarn die Heilige em 1221, em Eisenach, Thüringen, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 11 de setembro de 1227, em Otranto, Terra d'Otranto, Puglia, Itália, com 26 anos, e foi sepultado em Thüringen, Alemanha.

ru.Warburg, Hochstift Paderborn, Holy Roman: варбург, хочстифт падирборн, хилигесь рoмищесь раич
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ludwig IV von Thüringen родился 28 октября 1200 г. в местности креузбург, вартбургкрис, тuринген, Германия, его отцу по имени Hermann I. von Thüringen было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Sophia von Wittelsbach, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Landgräfin Elisabeth von Ungarn die Heilige в 1221 г. в местности аисенач, тuринген, Германия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 11 сентября 1227 г. в местности отранто, тирра д'отранто, пугля, Италия в возрасте 26 лет и был похоронен в местности тuринген, Германия.
